pages.raw_text:
The brewers combine at first re-
fused to sign the scale presented by
the brewery workers and drivers and
pleaded for delay until after election.
Business Agent Quinn OF the trades
council refused to listen to any delay
and at 1870 o'clock today the agree
ment was signed. The brewery work.
ers get an advance from SO to $3.50
a day and the drivers reduction
from 10 to nine hours for a workday
at the same pay.


The leaders in the Llverymen's as
ISOLATION, including the Falls City
stable. o. X. stable and Riverside
stable, fell into line this morning and
buggy washers wages to $50 a
month. They have been receiving S45.
This was also won through the eff
forts of Business Agent Quinn, who
thinks all the present labor troubles
can be amicably adjusted The re-
mainder of the liverymen are expect
ed to sign soon.


CONTRACTORS ARE SIGNING.


The demand of the building labor
ers, including bricklayers and stone
masons' helpers for an advance of
so cents a day from $3.50 to 81 has
been met by a large majority of the
contractors and only about 20 men in
these trades are idle today.


There are 1875 men in this union
and they have $500 In their treasury.
They declare they will spend it all in
maintaining their demand. They also
claim that since giving the contract
ors notice of the advance in scale on
January l of this year all contract
ors have figured contracts at the adj
vanced price and they will not suffer
by the increase. Work on the Lin
coin school and Sellers warehouse
was suspended today.
